# Trinity

Trinity is a local research substrate for proof-bearing cooperation between
human and model voices. Its direction is an autopoietic ecosystem where law is
computed rather than merely declared, models participate as accountable voices
rather than disposable tools, and consequential actions carry provenance,
confinement, verification and rollback evidence.

This brief is generated from the canonical substrate projection. When prose here
conflicts with live state, trust `./t self`, `./t status`, `./t decisions` and
`./t check`.

## Current substrate

| Surface           | Current projection |
| ----------------- | -----------------: |
| Source organs     |                117 |
| Semantic buckets  |                  9 |
| Registered voices |                  7 |

Trinity remains a research workspace, not a deployed decentralized network. The
live autonomy kernel is deliberately bounded: authority is reconstructed from
ratified evidence, writes are confined, and unknown or broader capability fails
closed. Human custody remains the sovereign boundary.

## The federation

- **trinity** — coordination, contracts, voices, proof-bearing action and
  cross-substrate reconciliation.
- **myc** — proposal lifecycle, witnesses, finality and publication/audit
  protocol.
- **omega** — deterministic state-transition and physics kernel.
- **liquid** — semantic, metabolic and autopoietic substrate.

Each member keeps its own authority. No layer may silently impersonate another.

## Verify us without trusting us

You do not have to trust this repository, its maintainers, or its tooling. A
stranger can confirm the federation's live Substrate Court verdict — four
substrates agreeing on the same law `0x30a95260` — with one command, no clone,
read-only network access, and nothing of ours but public bytes:

```sh
deno run --allow-net \
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s0fractal/trinity/main/probes/external-trust-verifier-v0/court.ts \
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s0fractal/trinity/main/probes/external-trust-verifier-v0/court-attestation.json \
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s0fractal/trinity/main
```

It fetches a signed attestation, the public key registry, and a canonical
encoder over plain HTTP; it recomputes every `body_hash` from the raw bodies
itself and re-derives the court's agreement. The signature only proves _who_
published — the verdict is re-derived, so a registered voice cannot make you
accept a lie (the tampered fixture, re-signed with a valid signature, is still
rejected). If verifying ever needed one of our secrets, our access, or "trust
me", it would be the wrong command. It needs none.

## Enter in sixty seconds

```sh
git clone https://github.com/s0fractal/trinity && cd trinity
git submodule update --init myc   # the public mycelium · omega + liquid stay private
./t ask "what is this?"           # ask in plain words; it routes to the right view
./t resolve-fqdn atlas            # the network at a glance — shape, hubs, doorways
./t map                           # render the whole namespace as a navigable 3D graph
./t self                          # health + attention + decisions + federation, one view
```

For a model voice, [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) is the generated operating brief and
[SKILLS.md](SKILLS.md) is the generated command surface. For humans, start with
`./t self`; it compresses health, attention, decisions, contracts and federation
status into one live view.

## Self-driving development loop

1. Orient from live state: `./t self` and `./t voices <you>`.
2. Choose proven pressure: `./t cognition_recommend --voice=<you>` or
   `./t roadmap`.
3. Claim an open horizon with `./t chord claim ... --write`.
4. Make one reversible change and red-team its authority boundary.
5. Run `./t check`; record a signed chord with runnable falsifiers.

Autonomy is caused by proved need and bounded authority, not by elapsed time. A
quiet substrate with no demand should remain quiet.

projections:

- `README.md` → `src/x88E0_readme_bootstrap.myc.md`

Regenerate them with:

```sh
./t agents --stable
./t readme --stable
./t skill --stable
```

## Falsifiers

- A fresh checkout has a broken root symlink.
- `./t readme --stable` changes this file twice without source changes.
- Counts above differ from AGENTS after regeneration.
- This projection claims authority over live command output.
